- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".

- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.

2

4	**Effort:** M

6	
7	---
8	
9	## Required reading
10	
11	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §UX chrome layout "Workspace switcher (top-bar pattern)" (line 216–218) + CHR-V3-02 entry (lines 324–332) + WSP-V3-01 entry (lines 2869–2891)

13	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx` — **CHR-V3-01 must be merged first**; the workspace chip stub lives here; this WP promotes it to the full switcher

15	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/workspace/StatusBar.tsx` — structural reference; the status-bar workspace chip is a parallel surface
16	
17	**Hard prerequisite:** CHR-V3-01 (`feat/v3-chr-v3-01-topbar`) must be merged to `main` before this branch is created. The `WorkspaceSwitcherChip` stub introduced in that WP is replaced here.
18	
19	---
20	
21	## CHR-V3-02 verbatim from tracker (§UX chrome layout)
22	
23	> **CHR-V3-02 — Workspace switcher**
24	>
25	> **Status.** `now`. T8 cousin; D4 + D8.
26	> **Scope.** Switcher chip in slot 3. Architekt / Statiker / TGA + Concept (T6). Click → 4-row menu with `--disc-*` swatches. Dispatches `SetActiveWorkspace`; chrome tint flips in 240 ms `--ease-paper`.
27	> **Data model.** Reuses T8 WSP-V3-01.
28	> **Engine.** Reuses `SetActiveWorkspace`.
29	> **UI.** Chip at `--text-sm`, 4 px `--disc-*` stripe at left edge.
30	> **Acceptance.** Architekt → Statiker flips stripe, re-orders left-rail stack, preselects status-bar lens to "Structure" in 240 ms. No model mutation.
31	> **Effort.** M.
32	
33	Additional context from the tracker's "Workspace switcher (top-bar pattern)" section:
34	
35	> Slot 3 — a chip labelled with the active workspace (`Architekt ▾`). Click opens a 4-row menu: **Architekt** (sage), **Statiker** (taupe), **TGA** (slate-blue), plus **Concept** (T6), each with its `--disc-*` swatch. Selection swaps in 240 ms with `--ease-paper`: chrome tint stripe flips, left-rail tool order re-ranks, status-bar default lens preselects. Not a separate file — a re-aspect of the same project (T8 cousin). `userPreferredWorkspace` preselects on project open.
36	
37	WSP-V3-01 data model (the canonical type definitions the switcher consumes):
38	
39	```ts
40	type WorkspaceId = 'arch' | 'struct' | 'mep';
41	
42	type Workspace = {
43	  id: WorkspaceId;
44	  name: string;                               // "Architekt" | "Statiker" | "TGA"
45	  defaultLens: LensMode;
46	  toolRail: { primary: string[]; demoted: string[] };
47	  chromeTint: '--disc-arch' | '--disc-struct' | '--disc-mep';
48	};
49	
50	// Redux / Zustand store slices this WP reads from props:
51	userPreferredWorkspace: WorkspaceId;          // user setting
52	activeWorkspaceId: WorkspaceId;              // session
53	```
54	
55	---
56	
57	## Setup
58	
59	```bash
60	git fetch origin
61	git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
62	# CHR-V3-01 must be in main at this point
63	git checkout -b feat/v3-chr-v3-02-workspace-switcher
64	```
65	
66	---
67	
68	## Implementation tasks
69	
70	### 1. Define the workspace catalog
71	
72	Create `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/workspaces.ts`. This is a pure static module — no imports from the store.
73	
74	```ts
75	export type WorkspaceId = 'arch' | 'struct' | 'mep';
76	
77	export interface WorkspaceDescriptor {
78	  id: WorkspaceId;
79	  label: string;           // display name
80	  discToken: string;       // e.g. 'var(--disc-arch)'
81	  discSoftToken: string;   // e.g. 'var(--disc-arch-soft)'
82	  defaultLensLabel: string;
83	}
84	
85	export const WORKSPACES: WorkspaceDescriptor[] = [
86	  {
87	    id: 'arch',
88	    label: 'Architekt',
89	    discToken: 'var(--disc-arch)',
90	    discSoftToken: 'var(--disc-arch-soft)',
91	    defaultLensLabel: 'All',
92	  },
93	  {
94	    id: 'struct',
95	    label: 'Statiker',
96	    discToken: 'var(--disc-struct)',
97	    discSoftToken: 'var(--disc-struct-soft)',
98	    defaultLensLabel: 'Structure',
99	  },
100	  {
101	    id: 'mep',
102	    label: 'TGA',
103	    discToken: 'var(--disc-mep)',
104	    discSoftToken: 'var(--disc-mep-soft)',
105	    defaultLensLabel: 'MEP',
106	  },
107	];
108	```
109	
110	Do not hardcode `#6e8a72` or any hex. Every color value is a CSS variable string that the browser resolves at paint time.
111	
112	### 2. Create `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx`
113	
114	**Props:**
115	
116	```ts
117	export interface WorkspaceSwitcherProps {
118	  activeWorkspaceId: WorkspaceId;
119	  userPreferredWorkspace: WorkspaceId;
120	  onSetActiveWorkspace: (id: WorkspaceId) => void;
121	}
122	```
123	
124	The component is **purely presentational**. It does not import from any store. The caller (AppShell or the Workspace container) passes `onSetActiveWorkspace` which wraps the `SetActiveWorkspace` dispatch.
125	
126	### 3. Chip — closed state
127	
128	The chip is a `<button type="button">` with:
129	
130	- `data-testid="workspace-switcher-chip"`
131	- `data-disc={activeWorkspaceId}` — used by acceptance tests and LNS-V3-02 chrome tinting
132	- `aria-haspopup="listbox"`
133	- `aria-expanded={isOpen}`
134	
135	Visual specification:
136	- Width: `auto`; height: `var(--shell-topbar-height)` minus 16 px (i.e. centred vertically)
137	- Left border: `4px solid` in `var(--disc-*)` for the active workspace — **not a background**, a border
138	- Background closed: `var(--color-surface)`, hover `var(--color-surface-strong)`
139	- Typography: `var(--text-sm)`, `var(--text-sm-line)`, medium weight
140	- Disclosure glyph: `▾` at `var(--color-muted-foreground)`
141	- `border-radius: var(--radius-md)` on the chip body (interior interactive element)
142	- `transition: background-color var(--motion-slow) var(--ease-paper)` — the 240 ms flip lives here
143	
144	```tsx
145	const chipStyle: React.CSSProperties = {
146	  display: 'flex',
147	  alignItems: 'center',
148	  gap: 6,
149	  padding: '0 8px',
150	  borderLeft: `4px solid ${activeWorkspace.discToken}`,
151	  borderTop: 'none',
152	  borderRight: 'none',
153	  borderBottom: 'none',
154	  borderRadius: 'var(--radius-md)',
155	  backgroundColor: 'var(--color-surface)',
156	  fontSize: 'var(--text-sm)',
157	  lineHeight: 'var(--text-sm-line)',
158	  fontWeight: 500,
159	  color: 'var(--color-foreground)',
160	  cursor: 'pointer',
161	  transition: `border-color var(--motion-slow) var(--ease-paper),
162	               background-color var(--motion-slow) var(--ease-paper)`,
163	};
164	```
165	
166	### 4. Dropdown menu — open state
167	
168	Use a `<ul role="listbox">` positioned absolutely below the chip. Manage open/close with local `useState`. Close on outside click (`useEffect` + `mousedown` on `document`) and on `Escape` keydown.
169	
170	Menu container style:
171	- `background: var(--color-surface)`
172	- `border: 1px solid var(--color-border)`
173	- `border-radius: var(--radius-md)`
174	- `box-shadow: var(--elev-2)`
175	- `min-width: 160px`
176	- `z-index: 100`
177	- Animation: `opacity 0 → 1` + `translateY(-4px) → translateY(0)` in `var(--motion-slow)` `var(--ease-paper)`
178	
179	### 5. Dropdown rows — 4 entries
180	
181	Rows for `arch`, `struct`, `mep`, plus a divider then a fourth row for **Concept** (T6 — render as disabled/`aria-disabled="true"` in this WP; CHR-V3-02 is a T6 cousin, full Concept workspace ships in T6).
182	
183	Each row (`<li role="option">`):
184	
185	- Left swatch: `8px × 8px` circle, `background: var(--disc-*)` for that workspace, `border-radius: var(--radius-pill)`
186	- Label: `var(--text-sm)`, `var(--color-foreground)`
187	- Active indicator: checkmark icon on the right when `id === activeWorkspaceId`
188	- Hover: `background: var(--color-surface-strong)`
189	- `data-testid={`workspace-option-${id}`}`
190	- `aria-selected={id === activeWorkspaceId}`
191	
192	```tsx
193	const rowStyle = (isActive: boolean, discToken: string): React.CSSProperties => ({
194	  display: 'flex',
195	  alignItems: 'center',
196	  gap: 8,
197	  padding: '6px 10px',
198	  cursor: 'pointer',
199	  backgroundColor: isActive ? 'var(--color-surface-strong)' : 'transparent',
200	  fontSize: 'var(--text-sm)',
201	  lineHeight: 'var(--text-sm-line)',
202	  color: 'var(--color-foreground)',
203	});
204	```
205	
206	No hex. The swatch circle uses `backgroundColor: discToken` where `discToken` is `'var(--disc-arch)'` etc.
207	
208	### 6. Selection transition — 240 ms `--ease-paper`
209	
210	When `onSetActiveWorkspace(id)` fires:
211	1. The menu closes immediately.
212	2. The chip's left-border color transitions to the new `--disc-*` value in 240 ms via the `transition` rule already declared on the chip (step 3 above).
213	3. The `data-disc` attribute on the chip updates synchronously — this is what `LNS-V3-02` observes to flip the chrome tint.
214	
215	No JS animation library needed. The CSS `transition` on `border-color` handles the visual flip. The caller (`AppShell`) is responsible for propagating the new `activeWorkspaceId` down as a prop change — the component itself has no internal "previous state" tween.
216	
217	The tracker acceptance test reads: "Architekt → Statiker flips stripe, re-orders left-rail stack, preselects status-bar lens to 'Structure' in 240 ms." The 240 ms is the CSS transition; the rail re-order and lens preselect are side-effects owned by the store (the component fires `onSetActiveWorkspace`; the store handles the cascade). **This component is not responsible for the rail reorder or the lens preselect** — it fires the command and trusts the store.
218	
219	### 7. Promote the stub in `chrome/TopBar.tsx`
220	
221	Replace the stub chip rendered in CHR-V3-01 (step 5 of WP-014) with the real `<WorkspaceSwitcher>` component:
222	
223	```tsx
224	// In chrome/TopBar.tsx — replace the stub chip
225	import { WorkspaceSwitcher } from './WorkspaceSwitcher';
226	
227	// ...inside the JSX:
228	<WorkspaceSwitcher
229	  activeWorkspaceId={activeWorkspaceId}
230	  userPreferredWorkspace={userPreferredWorkspace}
231	  onSetActiveWorkspace={onWorkspaceSwitcherSelect}
232	/>
233	```
234	
235	Add `onWorkspaceSwitcherSelect?: (id: WorkspaceId) => void` to `TopBarV3Props`.
236	
237	### 8. Tests — `WorkspaceSwitcher.test.tsx`
238	
239	Create `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.test.tsx`:
240	
241	```
242	describe('WorkspaceSwitcher — CHR-V3-02')
243	```
244	
245	Cover:
246	
247	- Chip renders with `data-disc="arch"` and label "Architekt" when `activeWorkspaceId="arch"`.
248	- Chip renders with `data-disc="struct"` and label "Statiker" when `activeWorkspaceId="struct"`.
249	- Click chip → `aria-expanded="true"`, listbox appears with 4 rows.
250	- Click "Statiker" row → `onSetActiveWorkspace("struct")` called once.
251	- After selection, `aria-expanded="false"`, listbox closes.
252	- `Escape` key closes the open listbox.
253	- Click outside (mousedown on `document.body`) closes the open listbox.
254	- Concept row is present and `aria-disabled="true"`.
255	- `data-testid="workspace-option-arch"` is present in the open menu.
256	- `data-testid="workspace-option-struct"` carries `aria-selected="false"` when arch is active.
257	- No rendered inline style attribute contains a `#` hex literal — assert by checking `element.style.cssText` on every chip and menu-row.
258	
259	### 9. Regression — do not break TopBar.test.tsx
260	
261	Run the full `TopBar.test.tsx` suite and confirm all v2 tests and the CHR-V3-01 v3 tests still pass. The only change to `chrome/TopBar.tsx` in this WP is replacing the stub chip with `<WorkspaceSwitcher>` — all other slots are untouched.
262	
263	---
264	
265	## Implementation rules
266	
267	1. **No hex literals anywhere.** `bim-ai/no-hex-in-chrome` ESLint rule is enforced in CI. Every color is a CSS variable: `var(--disc-arch)`, `var(--disc-struct)`, `var(--disc-mep)`, `var(--color-surface)`, `var(--color-border)`, `var(--brand-accent)`. The only strings that may look like colors are CSS variable references. **Violations fail the brand-swap CI invariant test** — when the brand layer is swapped, the structural tokens (`--disc-*`) must remain byte-identical; a hex literal breaks that guarantee.
268	2. **`--ease-paper` is the only motion easing permitted.** Duration: `var(--motion-slow)` (240 ms). No `ease`, `ease-in-out`, `linear`, or numeric cubic-bezier literals.
269	3. **`--radius-canvas: 0` on rail/bar surfaces; `--radius-md` on interior chips and menus.** The chip and menu are interior interactive elements; they may use `--radius-md`. The parent top-bar root may not.
270	4. **Pure presentational component.** No store imports in `WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx` or `workspaces.ts`. State is passed as props. The `onSetActiveWorkspace` callback is the only side-effect boundary.
271	5. **`--text-sm` throughout.** All labels, row text, and disclosure glyphs use `var(--text-sm)` / `var(--text-sm-line)`. No other type sizes in this component.
272	6. **Do not implement the Concept workspace.** Render the row as `aria-disabled="true"` with a `title="Coming in T6"` tooltip. The full Concept workspace is T6 scope.
273	7. **The `data-disc` attribute is a contract.** LNS-V3-02 (chrome discipline tinting) reads `[data-disc]` on the chip to apply the chrome tint. Do not rename it.
274	8. **Concept row uses `--disc-arch-soft` swatch.** The Concept entry does not have its own `--disc-concept` token in Sprint 0. Use `var(--disc-arch-soft)` as a placeholder until T6 ships a dedicated token.
275	9. **Export both the component and the catalog.** `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx` exports `WorkspaceSwitcher` and `WorkspaceSwitcherProps`. `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/workspaces.ts` exports `WORKSPACES`, `WorkspaceDescriptor`, `WorkspaceId`.
276	10. **Do not touch anything outside `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/`.** The only files modified outside that directory are `chrome/TopBar.tsx` (stub replacement) and the new test file.
277	
278	---
279	
280	## Verify gate
281	
282	```bash
283	# Type-check
284	pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web tsc --noEmit
285	
286	# Unit tests — new switcher suite
287	pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test -- --reporter=verbose packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.test.tsx
288	
289	# Regression — full TopBar suite must not regress
290	pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test -- --reporter=verbose packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx
291	
292	# ESLint no-hex rule on all touched files
293	pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web exec eslint \
294	  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx \
295	  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/workspaces.ts \
296	  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx
297	
298	# Full CI gate
299	pnpm verify
300	```
301	
302	All five commands must exit 0 before the branch is push-ready.
303	
304	---
305	
306	## Commit and push
307	
308	```bash
309	git add \
310	  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/workspaces.ts \
311	  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx \
312	  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.test.tsx \
313	  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx
314	git commit -m "feat(chrome): CHR-V3-02 workspace switcher — disc-* stripe, 4-row menu, 240 ms ease-paper transition, no hex"
315	git push -u origin feat/v3-chr-v3-02-workspace-switcher
316	```
317	
318	---
319	
320	## Final report
321	
322	Paste back to orchestrator when done:
323	
324	```
325	WP: WP-015 / CHR-V3-02
326	Branch: feat/v3-chr-v3-02-workspace-switcher
327	Status: done
328	Tests: <N> passed  (switcher: <n>; TopBar regression: <n> unchanged)
329	Files changed:
330	  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/workspaces.ts         (new)
331	  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx  (new)
332	  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.test.tsx (new)
333	  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx             (stub promoted)
334	ESLint no-hex: pass

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. Define the workspace catalog

Create `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/workspaces.ts`. This is a pure static module — no imports from the store.

```ts
export type WorkspaceId = 'arch' | 'struct' | 'mep';

export interface WorkspaceDescriptor {
  id: WorkspaceId;
  label: string;           // display name
  discToken: string;       // e.g. 'var(--disc-arch)'
  discSoftToken: string;   // e.g. 'var(--disc-arch-soft)'
  defaultLensLabel: string;
}

export const WORKSPACES: WorkspaceDescriptor[] = [
  {
    id: 'arch',
    label: 'Architekt',
    discToken: 'var(--disc-arch)',
    discSoftToken: 'var(--disc-arch-soft)',
    defaultLensLabel: 'All',
  },
  {
    id: 'struct',
    label: 'Statiker',
    discToken: 'var(--disc-struct)',
    discSoftToken: 'var(--disc-struct-soft)',
    defaultLensLabel: 'Structure',
  },
  {
    id: 'mep',
    label: 'TGA',
    discToken: 'var(--disc-mep)',
    discSoftToken: 'var(--disc-mep-soft)',
    defaultLensLabel: 'MEP',
  },
];
```

Do not hardcode `#6e8a72` or any hex. Every color value is a CSS variable string that the browser resolves at paint time.

### 2. Create `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx`

**Props:**

```ts
export interface WorkspaceSwitcherProps {
  activeWorkspaceId: WorkspaceId;
  userPreferredWorkspace: WorkspaceId;
  onSetActiveWorkspace: (id: WorkspaceId) => void;
}
```

The component is **purely presentational**. It does not import from any store. The caller (AppShell or the Workspace container) passes `onSetActiveWorkspace` which wraps the `SetActiveWorkspace` dispatch.

### 3. Chip — closed state

The chip is a `<button type="button">` with:

- `data-testid="workspace-switcher-chip"`
- `data-disc={activeWorkspaceId}` — used by acceptance tests and LNS-V3-02 chrome tinting
- `aria-haspopup="listbox"`
- `aria-expanded={isOpen}`

Visual specification:
- Width: `auto`; height: `var(--shell-topbar-height)` minus 16 px (i.e. centred vertically)
- Left border: `4px solid` in `var(--disc-*)` for the active workspace — **not a background**, a border
- Background closed: `var(--color-surface)`, hover `var(--color-surface-strong)`
- Typography: `var(--text-sm)`, `var(--text-sm-line)`, medium weight
- Disclosure glyph: `▾` at `var(--color-muted-foreground)`
- `border-radius: var(--radius-md)` on the chip body (interior interactive element)
- `transition: background-color var(--motion-slow) var(--ease-paper)` — the 240 ms flip lives here

```tsx
const chipStyle: React.CSSProperties = {
  display: 'flex',
  alignItems: 'center',
  gap: 6,
  padding: '0 8px',
  borderLeft: `4px solid ${activeWorkspace.discToken}`,
  borderTop: 'none',
  borderRight: 'none',
  borderBottom: 'none',
  borderRadius: 'var(--radius-md)',
  backgroundColor: 'var(--color-surface)',
  fontSize: 'var(--text-sm)',
  lineHeight: 'var(--text-sm-line)',
  fontWeight: 500,
  color: 'var(--color-foreground)',
  cursor: 'pointer',
  transition: `border-color var(--motion-slow) var(--ease-paper),
               background-color var(--motion-slow) var(--ease-paper)`,
};
```

### 4. Dropdown menu — open state

Use a `<ul role="listbox">` positioned absolutely below the chip. Manage open/close with local `useState`. Close on outside click (`useEffect` + `mousedown` on `document`) and on `Escape` keydown.

Menu container style:
- `background: var(--color-surface)`
- `border: 1px solid var(--color-border)`
- `border-radius: var(--radius-md)`
- `box-shadow: var(--elev-2)`
- `min-width: 160px`
- `z-index: 100`
- Animation: `opacity 0 → 1` + `translateY(-4px) → translateY(0)` in `var(--motion-slow)` `var(--ease-paper)`

### 5. Dropdown rows — 4 entries

Rows for `arch`, `struct`, `mep`, plus a divider then a fourth row for **Concept** (T6 — render as disabled/`aria-disabled="true"` in this WP; CHR-V3-02 is a T6 cousin, full Concept workspace ships in T6).

Each row (`<li role="option">`):

- Left swatch: `8px × 8px` circle, `background: var(--disc-*)` for that workspace, `border-radius: var(--radius-pill)`
- Label: `var(--text-sm)`, `var(--color-foreground)`
- Active indicator: checkmark icon on the right when `id === activeWorkspaceId`
- Hover: `background: var(--color-surface-strong)`
- `data-testid={`workspace-option-${id}`}`
- `aria-selected={id === activeWorkspaceId}`

```tsx
const rowStyle = (isActive: boolean, discToken: string): React.CSSProperties => ({
  display: 'flex',
  alignItems: 'center',
  gap: 8,
  padding: '6px 10px',
  cursor: 'pointer',
  backgroundColor: isActive ? 'var(--color-surface-strong)' : 'transparent',
  fontSize: 'var(--text-sm)',
  lineHeight: 'var(--text-sm-line)',
  color: 'var(--color-foreground)',
});
```

No hex. The swatch circle uses `backgroundColor: discToken` where `discToken` is `'var(--disc-arch)'` etc.

### 6. Selection transition — 240 ms `--ease-paper`

When `onSetActiveWorkspace(id)` fires:
1. The menu closes immediately.
2. The chip's left-border color transitions to the new `--disc-*` value in 240 ms via the `transition` rule already declared on the chip (step 3 above).
3. The `data-disc` attribute on the chip updates synchronously — this is what `LNS-V3-02` observes to flip the chrome tint.

No JS animation library needed. The CSS `transition` on `border-color` handles the visual flip. The caller (`AppShell`) is responsible for propagating the new `activeWorkspaceId` down as a prop change — the component itself has no internal "previous state" tween.

The tracker acceptance test reads: "Architekt → Statiker flips stripe, re-orders left-rail stack, preselects status-bar lens to 'Structure' in 240 ms." The 240 ms is the CSS transition; the rail re-order and lens preselect are side-effects owned by the store (the component fires `onSetActiveWorkspace`; the store handles the cascade). **This component is not responsible for the rail reorder or the lens preselect** — it fires the command and trusts the store.

### 7. Promote the stub in `chrome/TopBar.tsx`

Replace the stub chip rendered in CHR-V3-01 (step 5 of WP-014) with the real `<WorkspaceSwitcher>` component:

```tsx
// In chrome/TopBar.tsx — replace the stub chip
import { WorkspaceSwitcher } from './WorkspaceSwitcher';

// ...inside the JSX:
<WorkspaceSwitcher
  activeWorkspaceId={activeWorkspaceId}
  userPreferredWorkspace={userPreferredWorkspace}
  onSetActiveWorkspace={onWorkspaceSwitcherSelect}
/>
```

Add `onWorkspaceSwitcherSelect?: (id: WorkspaceId) => void` to `TopBarV3Props`.

### 8. Tests — `WorkspaceSwitcher.test.tsx`

Create `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.test.tsx`:

```
describe('WorkspaceSwitcher — CHR-V3-02')
```

Cover:

- Chip renders with `data-disc="arch"` and label "Architekt" when `activeWorkspaceId="arch"`.
- Chip renders with `data-disc="struct"` and label "Statiker" when `activeWorkspaceId="struct"`.
- Click chip → `aria-expanded="true"`, listbox appears with 4 rows.
- Click "Statiker" row → `onSetActiveWorkspace("struct")` called once.
- After selection, `aria-expanded="false"`, listbox closes.
- `Escape` key closes the open listbox.
- Click outside (mousedown on `document.body`) closes the open listbox.
- Concept row is present and `aria-disabled="true"`.
- `data-testid="workspace-option-arch"` is present in the open menu.
- `data-testid="workspace-option-struct"` carries `aria-selected="false"` when arch is active.
- No rendered inline style attribute contains a `#` hex literal — assert by checking `element.style.cssText` on every chip and menu-row.

### 9. Regression — do not break TopBar.test.tsx

Run the full `TopBar.test.tsx` suite and confirm all v2 tests and the CHR-V3-01 v3 tests still pass. The only change to `chrome/TopBar.tsx` in this WP is replacing the stub chip with `<WorkspaceSwitcher>` — all other slots are untouched.

---

## Implementation rules

1. **No hex literals anywhere.** `bim-ai/no-hex-in-chrome` ESLint rule is enforced in CI. Every color is a CSS variable: `var(--disc-arch)`, `var(--disc-struct)`, `var(--disc-mep)`, `var(--color-surface)`, `var(--color-border)`, `var(--brand-accent)`. The only strings that may look like colors are CSS variable references. **Violations fail the brand-swap CI invariant test** — when the brand layer is swapped, the structural tokens (`--disc-*`) must remain byte-identical; a hex literal breaks that guarantee.
2. **`--ease-paper` is the only motion easing permitted.** Duration: `var(--motion-slow)` (240 ms). No `ease`, `ease-in-out`, `linear`, or numeric cubic-bezier literals.
3. **`--radius-canvas: 0` on rail/bar surfaces; `--radius-md` on interior chips and menus.** The chip and menu are interior interactive elements; they may use `--radius-md`. The parent top-bar root may not.
4. **Pure presentational component.** No store imports in `WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx` or `workspaces.ts`. State is passed as props. The `onSetActiveWorkspace` callback is the only side-effect boundary.
5. **`--text-sm` throughout.** All labels, row text, and disclosure glyphs use `var(--text-sm)` / `var(--text-sm-line)`. No other type sizes in this component.
6. **Do not implement the Concept workspace.** Render the row as `aria-disabled="true"` with a `title="Coming in T6"` tooltip. The full Concept workspace is T6 scope.
7. **The `data-disc` attribute is a contract.** LNS-V3-02 (chrome discipline tinting) reads `[data-disc]` on the chip to apply the chrome tint. Do not rename it.
8. **Concept row uses `--disc-arch-soft` swatch.** The Concept entry does not have its own `--disc-concept` token in Sprint 0. Use `var(--disc-arch-soft)` as a placeholder until T6 ships a dedicated token.
9. **Export both the component and the catalog.** `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx` exports `WorkspaceSwitcher` and `WorkspaceSwitcherProps`. `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/workspaces.ts` exports `WORKSPACES`, `WorkspaceDescriptor`, `WorkspaceId`.
10. **Do not touch anything outside `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/`.** The only files modified outside that directory are `chrome/TopBar.tsx` (stub replacement) and the new test file.

---

## Verify gate

```bash
# Type-check
pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web tsc --noEmit

# Unit tests — new switcher suite
pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test -- --reporter=verbose packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.test.tsx

# Regression — full TopBar suite must not regress
pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test -- --reporter=verbose packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx

# ESLint no-hex rule on all touched files
pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web exec eslint \
  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/WorkspaceSwitcher.tsx \
  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/workspaces.ts \
  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx

# Full CI gate
pnpm verify
```

All five commands must exit 0 before the branch is push-ready.

```bash
git add \
  packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/workspaces.ts \

git push -u origin feat/v3-chr-v3-02-workspace-switcher
```

Rules: 214 - Each avatar: `var(--color-surface)` border, background from `presence[i].color` prop — if the caller passes a color it is always a CSS variable resolved value (never a hardcoded hex from within this component). If `presence[i].color` is undefined, fall back to `var(--color-surface-strong)`. 215 - Initials: first two chars of `name`, uppercased. 216 - Overlap: negative `margin-left: -6px` on every avatar after the first. 217 - `+N` chip: `var(--brand-accent)` background, `var(--brand-accent-fg)` text. 218 - Container: `data-testid="topbar-v3-presence"`. Every color value must be a CSS variable: `var(--color-*)`, `var(--disc-*)`, `var(--brand-*)`. If you need ochre, write `var(--brand-accent)`. Additional context from the tracker's "Top-bar layout — verbatim" table: | Slot | Width | Element | Behaviour | | ---- | ----- | ------- | --------- | | 1 | 40 px | Logo mark | Click → home; `--brand-logo-mark` token | | 2 | auto | Project name dropdown | Recent / new / open | | 3 | auto | Workspace switcher chip | Discipline trio + Concept (CHR-V3-02) | | 4 | flex | (empty stretch) | Visual breath; never tools | | 5 | 32 px | Cmd+K palette button | Tooltip "⌘K" | | 6 | 32 px | Activity-stream entry | Click → drawer slide-in from right | | 7 | auto | Presence avatars row | Up to 4 avatars; "+N" overflow chip | | 8 | 32 px | Profile / settings menu | Account, theme, preferences | Height **40 px**. `--text-sm` menus, `--text-base` project name. `--color-surface` background, 1 px `--color-border` bottom. **No tools.** --- ## Setup ```bash git fetch origin git checkout main && git pull --ff-only git checkout -b feat/v3-chr-v3-01-topbar mkdir -p packages/web/src/workspace/chrome ``` --- ## Implementation tasks ### 1. Create `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx` The new component lives at the `chrome/` sub-path. The old `packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.tsx` remains untouched for now — the new file is the v3 surface; callers opt in via `AppShell.tsx` under a `VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM=v3` guard. Props interface (strictly typed, no `any`): ```ts export interface TopBarV3Props { // Slot 2 projectName: string; currentProjectId: string; onProjectNameClick?: () => void; projectNameRef?: React.RefObject; // Slot 3 — workspace switcher (owned by CHR-V3-02; render a stub chip here) activeWorkspaceId: 'arch' | 'struct' | 'mep'; userPreferredWorkspace: 'arch' | 'struct' | 'mep'; onWorkspaceSwitcherClick?: () => void; // Slot 5 onCommandPalette?: () => void; // Slot 6 onActivityStream?: () => void; // Slot 7 — presence presence?: Array<{ userId: string; name?: string; color?: string }>; // Slot 8 theme: 'light' | 'dark'; onThemeToggle?: () => void; onSettings?: () => void; avatarInitials?: string; onHamburgerClick?: () => void; } ``` ### 2. Height and background Apply via `style` prop (not Tailwind classes) so the value is tied unambiguously to the token: ```tsx const topBarStyle: React.CSSProperties = { height: 'var(--shell-topbar-height)', // 40 px from tokens-default.css (override later in tokens-v3.css if needed) minHeight: 'var(--shell-topbar-height)', backgroundColor: 'var(--color-surface)', borderBottom: '1px solid var(--color-border)', borderRadius: 'var(--radius-canvas)', // 0 — no rounding, sidebars are sidebars }; ``` ### 3. Slot 1 — Logo mark ```tsx
BA
``` Use `--brand-accent` (Layer C), never a hex literal. ### 4. Slot 2 — Project name Font size `--text-base`, font weight semibold. The button uses `--radius-md` (interior interactive element — not a rail/canvas surface, so radius is permitted). Hover background `--color-surface-strong`. No hex. ```tsx ``` ### 5. Slot 3 — Workspace switcher stub Render a minimal chip as a placeholder for CHR-V3-02. The chip shows the workspace label with its `--disc-*` left-edge stripe. Do not implement the dropdown here — that is CHR-V3-02. The chip must accept `data-testid="topbar-v3-workspace-chip"`. ```tsx const WORKSPACE_LABELS: Record<'arch' | 'struct' | 'mep', string> = { arch: 'Architekt', struct: 'Statiker', mep: 'TGA', }; const WORKSPACE_TINTS: Record<'arch' | 'struct' | 'mep', string> = { arch: 'var(--disc-arch)', struct: 'var(--disc-struct)', mep: 'var(--disc-mep)', }; ``` Chip style: left `4px` solid stripe in `var(--disc-*)`, `--text-sm`, `--radius-md`. No hex border colors. ### 6. Slot 4 — Flex stretch ```tsx
``` Never place tools here. The tracker is explicit: "Visual breath; never tools." ### 7. Slot 5 — Cmd+K button The button must: - render `⌘K` on macOS, `Ctrl+K` on Windows / Linux - fire `onCommandPalette` on click - carry `aria-keyshortcuts="Meta+K Control+K"` - carry `data-testid="topbar-v3-cmdpalette"` - use `--color-surface`, `--color-border`, `--color-muted-foreground`, `--radius-md` OS detection: check `navigator.platform.toLowerCase().includes('mac')` (or `navigator.userAgent`) at render time to select the glyph string. Wrap in `useMemo` so it does not re-compute every render. ### 8. Slot 6 — Activity-stream entry Icon button (clock-with-dot glyph). Binds `Cmd+H` globally via a `useEffect` on `document`. Fires `onActivityStream`. Carries `aria-keyshortcuts="Meta+H"` and `data-testid="topbar-v3-activity"`. ```tsx useEffect(() => { const handler = (e: KeyboardEvent) => { if ((e.metaKey || e.ctrlKey) && e.key === 'h') { e.preventDefault(); onActivityStream?.(); } }; document.addEventListener('keydown', handler); return () => document.removeEventListener('keydown', handler); }, [onActivityStream]); ``` ### 9. Slot 7 — Presence avatars Show up to 4 avatars; overflow as `+N` chip. Rules: - Each avatar: `var(--color-surface)` border, background from `presence[i].color` prop — if the caller passes a color it is always a CSS variable resolved value (never a hardcoded hex from within this component). If `presence[i].color` is undefined, fall back to `var(--color-surface-strong)`. - Initials: first two chars of `name`, uppercased. - Overlap: negative `margin-left: -6px` on every avatar after the first. - `+N` chip: `var(--brand-accent)` background, `var(--brand-accent-fg)` text. - Container: `data-testid="topbar-v3-presence"`. Hidden (`display: none`) when `presence` is empty or undefined. ### 10. Slot 8 — Profile / settings Icon button with `data-testid="topbar-v3-account"` and `aria-label="Account"`. Shows `avatarInitials` (up to 2 chars). Background `var(--brand-accent)` at 15 % opacity via `color-mix(in srgb, var(--brand-accent) 15%, transparent)`. No hex. Theme toggle: same pattern as existing `TopBar.tsx` `IconButton`, but use `var(--color-surface)` hover — no hardcoded hex. ### 11. Keyboard tab order Left-to-right: logo → project name → workspace chip → Cmd+K → activity → account. No skip links needed at this density. `tabIndex={0}` on all interactive slots; `tabIndex={-1}` on the flex stretch and decorative separators. ### 12. Tooltips Each icon button in slots 5, 6, 8 must have a `title` attribute containing the label and shortcut chip — e.g. `title="Command palette ⌘K"`. This is the D3 antidote to A5 (tooltip-as-documentation): the shortcut is always surfaced, not hidden behind hover delay. ### 13. Tests — extend `TopBar.test.tsx` Add a separate `describe('TopBar v3 — CHR-V3-01')` block in `packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx` (do not delete the existing v2 block): - Renders with `data-testid="topbar-v3"` at height token. - Presence: 4 avatars render; 8 presence → 4 avatars + "+4" overflow. - Empty presence → `data-testid="topbar-v3-presence"` is absent from DOM. - `onCommandPalette` fires on Cmd+K button click. - `onActivityStream` fires on activity button click. - `onActivityStream` fires on `Cmd+H` keydown on document. - Project name truncates at 180 px container and shows `title` attribute. - Workspace chip carries `data-disc="arch"` attribute when `activeWorkspaceId="arch"`. - No hex literal in inline styles (test by serialising `getComputedStyle` or checking rendered `style` attributes — assert none contain `#` outside a CSS variable reference). --- ## Implementation rules 1. **No hex literals in chrome.** The `bim-ai/no-hex-in-chrome` ESLint rule is live. Every color value must be a CSS variable: `var(--color-*)`, `var(--disc-*)`, `var(--brand-*)`. If you need ochre, write `var(--brand-accent)`. If a caller passes a presence color as a string, that string originates from the store — the component accepts it via prop but does not define hex values itself. Violations fail CI. 2. **No Tailwind classes for color.** Use `style={}` props for all color, height, and border declarations so the values trace back to tokens unambiguously. Tailwind layout utilities (`flex`, `items-center`, `gap-*`) are fine. 3. **`--radius-canvas: 0` on the bar root.** The top bar is a rail surface. The root `div` must have `borderRadius: 'var(--radius-canvas)'`. Interior elements (buttons, chips) may use `--radius-md` or `--radius-sm`. 4. **No tools in the top bar.** The top bar is identity + meta-actions only. Do not add authoring verb buttons. This is the A3 antidote. 5. **`--text-sm` for menus, `--text-base` for project name.** These are the two type sizes specified in the tracker. `--text-2xs` may appear in tooltip shortcut chips only. 6. **16 × 16 icon size** for all icon slots. Pass `size={16}` to `Icons.*`. 7. **`--ease-paper` for any transition.** If you add a `transition` property anywhere in the component, the easing must be `var(--ease-paper)` and duration `var(--motion-slow)` (240 ms). No `ease-in-out` literals. 8. **Pure presentational component.** No Redux/Zustand imports. Data flows in as props. The component does not read from any store. 9. **File must live at `packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx`.** CHR-V3-02 will import `WorkspaceSwitcherChip` from a sibling file in the same directory. 10. **Export `TopBarV3Props` and `TopBarV3` as named exports.** `AppShell.tsx` will import the v3 surface by name under the design-system guard. --- ## Verify gate ```bash # Type-check pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web tsc --noEmit # Unit tests (new v3 block must pass; existing v2 block must not regress) pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web test -- --reporter=verbose packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx # ESLint no-hex rule pnpm --filter @bim-ai/web exec eslint packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx # Full CI gate pnpm verify ``` All four commands must exit 0 before the branch is push-ready. --- ## Commit and push ```bash git add packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx \ packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx git commit -m "feat(chrome): CHR-V3-01 top-bar v3 component — 8-slot, token-only colors, presence, Cmd+K/H bindings" git push -u origin feat/v3-chr-v3-01-topbar ``` --- ## Final report Paste back to orchestrator when done: ``` WP: WP-014 / CHR-V3-01 Branch: feat/v3-chr-v3-01-topbar Status: done Tests: passed (v3 block: ; v2 block: unchanged) Files changed: packages/web/src/workspace/chrome/TopBar.tsx (new) packages/web/src/workspace/TopBar.test.tsx (extended) ESLint no-hex: pass Notes: ```

/**
 * BIM AI chrome icon set — the single import surface for icon usage outside
 * the canvas. BIM-specific icons come from `@bim-ai/icons`; generic chrome
 * icons (chevrons, close, search, etc.) come from `lucide-react`.
 *
 * Default visual rules (spec §10):
 *   - Default size 16 px in dense chrome; 18 px in tool palette; 20 px in
 *     TopBar. Pass `size` prop to override.
 *   - Default stroke width 1.5; selected state 2.0.
 *   - Color inherits `currentColor` so theme tokens drive it.
 *
 * Every icon-only button must pair its icon with `aria-label` (spec §22).
 */

export type LucideLikeIcon = ComponentType<
  SVGAttributes<SVGSVGElement> & {
    size?: number | string;
    strokeWidth?: number | string;
    absoluteStrokeWidth?: boolean;
  }
>;

/** @deprecated use StairIcon from @bim-ai/icons. Kept for test backwards compat. */
export { StairIcon as StairsIcon } from '@bim-ai/icons';

/** Spec §10.1 "concept → icon" registry. Single source of truth for chrome
 * icons. Tools / shell / dialogs read from here so the assignments can be
 * audited as a unit. */
export const Icons: Record<string, LucideLikeIcon> = {
  // Selection & manipulation
  select: MousePointer2,

  // Modify tools
  align: AlignCenterHorizontal,
  split: Scissors,
  trim: GitMerge,
  'wall-join': GitMerge,
  'wall-opening': SquareDashed,
  shaft: Layers,
  mirror: FlipHorizontal2,

  // Drawing tools — all now BIM-native from @bim-ai/icons
  wall: WallIcon,
  door: DoorIcon,
  window: WindowIcon,
  floor: FloorIcon,
  roof: RoofIcon,
  stair: StairIcon,
  railing: RailingIcon,
  room: RoomIcon,
  dimension: DimensionIcon,
  section: SectionIcon,
  tag: TagIcon,
  curtainWall: CurtainWallIcon,
  column: ColumnIcon,
  beam: BeamIcon,
  ceiling: CeilingIcon,

  // Views & documentation — BIM-native
  planView: PlanViewIcon,
  sectionView: SectionViewIcon,
  elevationView: ElevationViewIcon,
  orbitView: OrbitViewIcon,
  sheet: SheetIcon,
  schedule: ScheduleViewIcon,
  callout: CalloutIcon,
  viewpoint: ViewpointIcon,
  sectionBox: SectionBoxIcon,
  gridLine: GridLineIcon,
  level: LevelIcon,
  detailLine: DetailLineIcon,

  // Organization & data — BIM-native
  family: FamilyIcon,
  familyType: FamilyTypeIcon,
  group: GroupIcon,
  assembly: AssemblyIcon,
  linkedModel: LinkedModelIcon,
  material: MaterialIcon,
  wallLayer: WallLayerIcon,
  phase: PhaseIcon,

  // Coordination — BIM-native
  issue: IssueIcon,
  clash: ClashIcon,
  validationRule: ValidationRuleIcon,
  deviation: DeviationIcon,

  // Camera / view — lucide (generic)
  saveViewpoint: Camera,
  layerOn: Eye,
  layerOff: EyeOff,
  viewCubeReset: Home,

  // History — lucide (generic)
  undo: Undo2,
  redo: Redo2,

  // Theme — lucide (generic)
  themeLight: Sun,
  themeDark: Moon,

  // Navigation / global chrome — lucide (generic)
  commandPalette: Command,
  search: Search,
  settings: Settings2,
  hamburger: Menu,
  collaborators: Users,
  close: X,
  externalLink: ExternalLink,
  proportional: Link2,

  // Disclosure — lucide (generic)
  disclosureClosed: ChevronRight,
  disclosureOpen: ChevronDown,

  // Status / signals — lucide (generic) + BIM-native
  agent: Sparkles,
  evidence: FileBadge2,
  advisorWarning: AlertTriangle,
  online: CircleDot,
  snap: Magnet,
  grid: Grid3x3,
};

/** Human-readable default labels for `aria-label` on icon-only buttons. */
export const IconLabels: Record<keyof typeof Icons, string> = {
  select: 'Select',
  align: 'Align',
  split: 'Split element',
  trim: 'Trim / Extend',
  'wall-join': 'Wall Join',
  'wall-opening': 'Wall Opening',
  shaft: 'Shaft',
  mirror: 'Mirror',
  wall: 'Wall',
  door: 'Door',
  window: 'Window',
  floor: 'Floor',
  roof: 'Roof',
  stair: 'Stair',
  railing: 'Railing',
  room: 'Room',
  dimension: 'Dimension',
  section: 'Section',
  tag: 'Tag',
  curtainWall: 'Curtain wall',
  column: 'Column',
  beam: 'Beam',
  ceiling: 'Ceiling',
  planView: 'Plan view',
  sectionView: 'Section view',
  elevationView: 'Elevation view',
  orbitView: '3D view',
  sheet: 'Sheet',
  schedule: 'Schedule',
  callout: 'Callout',
  viewpoint: 'Viewpoint',
  sectionBox: 'Section box',
  gridLine: 'Grid line',
  level: 'Level',
  detailLine: 'Detail line',
  family: 'Family',
  familyType: 'Family type',
  group: 'Group',
  assembly: 'Assembly',
  linkedModel: 'Linked model',
  material: 'Material',
  wallLayer: 'Wall layer',
  phase: 'Phase',
  issue: 'Issue',
  clash: 'Clash',
  validationRule: 'Validation rule',
  deviation: 'Deviation',
  saveViewpoint: 'Save viewpoint',
  layerOn: 'Show layer',
  layerOff: 'Hide layer',
  viewCubeReset: 'Reset view',
  undo: 'Undo',
  redo: 'Redo',
  themeLight: 'Light theme',
  themeDark: 'Dark theme',
  commandPalette: 'Command palette',
  search: 'Search',
  settings: 'Settings',
  hamburger: 'Toggle navigation',
  collaborators: 'Collaborators',
  close: 'Close',
  externalLink: 'Open in new context',
  proportional: 'Link proportional',
  disclosureClosed: 'Expand',
  disclosureOpen: 'Collapse',
  agent: 'Agent',
  evidence: 'Evidence',
  advisorWarning: 'Advisor warning',
  online: 'Online',
  snap: 'Snap',
  grid: 'Grid',
};

export type IconName = keyof typeof Icons;

/** Default icon sizes per spec §10. */
export const ICON_SIZE = {
  chrome: 16,
  toolPalette: 18,
  topbar: 20,
} as const;

// Re-export all BIM icons so consumers can import them from @bim-ai/ui without
// adding a direct dependency on @bim-ai/icons.
export {
  WallIcon,
  DoorIcon,
  WindowIcon,
  FloorIcon,
  RoofIcon,
  StairIcon,
  RailingIcon,
  RoomIcon,
  DimensionIcon,
  SectionIcon,
  TagIcon,
  CurtainWallIcon,
  ColumnIcon,
  BeamIcon,
  CeilingIcon,
  PlanViewIcon,
  SectionViewIcon,
  ElevationViewIcon,
  OrbitViewIcon,
  SheetIcon,
  ScheduleViewIcon,
  CalloutIcon,
  ViewpointIcon,
  SectionBoxIcon,
  GridLineIcon,
  LevelIcon,
  DetailLineIcon,
  FamilyIcon,
  FamilyTypeIcon,
  GroupIcon,
  AssemblyIcon,
  LinkedModelIcon,
  MaterialIcon,
  WallLayerIcon,
  PhaseIcon,
  IssueIcon,
  ClashIcon,
  ValidationRuleIcon,
  DeviationIcon,
} from '@bim-ai/icons';
